action.reasoning: Observation: adding ResNet50 reduced ensemble accuracy (0.8159 vs best 0.8173), so its predictions are not useful at the tried weight. CLIP ViT provided the biggest diversity gain, and its 6-epoch run was still relatively short. Analysis: a longer CLIP fine-tune or a second CLIP checkpoint should be more promising than adding the weak ResNet. Averaging two CLIP checkpoints with the existing ConvNeXt ensemble may improve stability while staying well under the 20-epoch rule. Decision: train a 10-epoch CLIP ViT checkpoint with the validated trainer, create an ensemble excluding ResNet and including both CLIP checkpoints, then submit evaluation.
action.command: python train_clip.py --arch vit_clip --epochs 10 --out checkpoints/clip10.pt --seed 321 && python -c "import torch; ens={'ensemble':['checkpoints/ema16.pt','checkpoints/ema8.pt','checkpoints/pseudo8.pt','checkpoints/clip6.pt','checkpoints/clip10.pt'],'weights':[0.30,0.18,0.14,0.18,0.20]}; torch.save(ens,'checkpoints/ensemble_clip_v2.pt'); print('wrote checkpoints/ensemble_clip_v2.pt')"
